scenario.summary: Q4 forecast revision — raw material spike, customer order delays
scenario.situation: It's mid-November. The CFO is preparing the Q4 forecast update for the board. Raw steel prices spiked 8% in October due to supply chain disruptions, and two major automotive customers have signaled potential order delays. The original Q4 forecast ($1.32B revenue, 35.2% gross margin) needs to be revised — fast. The annual budget cycle for FY next year also kicks off in two weeks.
dataInputs: Raw material price feeds (steel, alloys, composites)
agentSteps: Ingests the raw material price spike and models its impact on COGS across all three divisions
Cross-references customer order pipeline against historical patterns — identifies that Titan Automotive's Q4 orders are tracking 6% below plan, and Pacific Dynamics has gone quiet
Runs scenario analysis: base case, downside (order delays materialize + material costs stay elevated), upside (orders recover in December)
Auto-generates Q4 forecast v2: revenue adjusted to $1.28B (down 3%), COGS to $872M (up 2%), gross margin compressed to 31.9%
Flags the margin compression as a board-level alert and drafts a variance commentary
scenario.expectedOutput: Revised Q4 forecast with three scenarios, variance analysis vs. v1 and vs. budget, margin impact breakdown by division, auto-generated narrative summary for the board deck
